summary: High‑energy 1985 outdoor field show with a crisp audience tape; standout Scarlet Fire, She Belongs to Me and an inspiring second‑set space → Scarlet Fire sequence.
review: The recording captures an exuberant afternoon at a small college field. Reviewers rave about the tight playing, especially the smoking Scarlet Fire/Space transition, heartfelt She Belongs to Me and a sweet Comes a Time, while noting the tape’s clear stereo mix that even outshines many soundboards. The only minor gripe is an initially low volume on the first set.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance and tape quality; show quality scores near 5 ★ and recording quality consistently described as clear and lively.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear stereo audience tape with crisp instrument separation; a few reviewers note the first‑set volume was low at the start, but overall mix is better than many soundboard releases.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic first set followed by an unusual placement of drums after Space; the second‑set space → Scarlet Fire transition is highlighted as a high point, with seamless segues into US Blues and Satisfaction that kept the vibe flowing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as wailing on the guitar and delivering high‑spirited, energetic leads throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bobby (Brent) is clearly audible and “playing up a storm”; one reviewer missed a hoped‑for Brent solo but overall keyboard work was praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums were noted for their placement after Space; some early low volume was corrected by the taper.
